english_text: Do not whistle at night
It attracts ghosts
Do not shake your legs
Shaking legs shakes off your fortune
Do not write names in red
Red ink means death
The number four means death
Many buildings skip the fourth floor
Do not cut nails at night
Cutting nails at night brings bad luck
Do not give shoes as a gift
The person will walk away from you
Dreaming of pigs means money
Dreaming of teeth falling out is bad
If your ear itches someone is talking about you
korean_text: 귀가 가려우면 누가 내 얘기 하는 거래
romanization: gwiga garyeoumyeon nuga nae yaegi haneun georae

english_text: If you see a magpie good news is coming
Crows bring bad news
Do not step on cracks
Fan death is a Korean belief
Do not sleep with the fan on all night
korean_text: 선풍기 켜고 자면 안 된다고 해
romanization: seonpunggi kyeogo jamyeon an doendago hae

english_text: Eating seaweed soup before an exam is bad luck
korean_text: 시험 전에 미역국 먹으면 재수 없어
romanization: siheom jeone miyeokguk meogeumyeon jaesu eopseo

english_text: Because seaweed is slippery meaning you will slip and fail
Eat sticky rice cakes before exams instead
Sticky things help you stick to success
Moving on a good day brings luck
People consult calendars for moving dates
Do not give a clock as a gift
Breaking a mirror brings seven years of bad luck
Black cats are unlucky
Putting chopsticks upright in rice is bad
It resembles incense for the dead
Do not pass food with chopsticks to chopsticks
korean_text: 젓가락으로 젓가락에 음식 전달하면 안 돼
romanization: jeotgarageuro jeotgarage eumsik jeondalhamyeon an dwae

english_text: This resembles a funeral ritual
Spilling salt is bad luck
Touching your nose means you will get money
If your palm itches money is coming
Sneezing means someone misses you
Do not open an umbrella indoors
Carrying lucky charms protects you
Throwing salt wards off evil
Visiting a shaman can change your luck
korean_text: 무당을 찾아가면 운이 바뀔 수 있대
romanization: mudangeul chajagamyeon uni bakkwil su itdae

english_text: Do not point at rainbows
Eating tteok on New Year brings good fortune
Sleeping with your head facing north is bad
korean_text: 북쪽으로 머리를 두고 자면 안 돼
romanization: bukjjogeuro meorireul dugo jamyeon an dwae

english_text: It is how the deceased are positioned
Do not pick up coins from the ground at night
korean_text: 밤에 땅에 있는 동전을 줍지 마
romanization: bame ttange inneun dongjeoneul jupji ma

english_text: If a butterfly enters your home guests are coming
Finding a four-leaf clover is very lucky
Do not trim your bangs before an important event
korean_text: 중요한 일 전에 앞머리 자르지 마
romanization: jungyohan il jeone apmeori jareuji ma

english_text: Seeing a shooting star means a wish will come true
Knocking on wood prevents bad luck
Superstitions are part of culture
Some superstitions have logical origins
Older generations take superstitions seriously
Young people mostly find them fun
Superstitions connect us to our ancestors
Every culture has its own superstitions
Do not take superstitions too seriously
It is fun to learn about them though
Korean superstitions reflect traditional values
